title: Council v2.6 — Security Hardening & Rate Limiting
priority: CRITICAL — must ship before any public launch or Product Hunt submission

context.product: Council v2.5 is live at https://councilpro.vercel.app. Verdict API uses Claude Sonnet (~$0.03-0.05/call). No rate limiting on the most expensive endpoint. Admin uses query-param auth. Events/feedback endpoints have zero auth.
context.validated_problem: Security audit on 2026-04-17 identified 5 exploitable gaps. Any of these could burn API credits, poison analytics, or leak admin data before/during launch.
root_causes: /api/verdict has ZERO rate limiting — TODO comment since v2.1, never implemented
/api/admin/stats authenticates via query param ?key=... — logged in browser history, proxies, access logs
/api/events accepts any payload with no auth — anyone can forge analytics events
/api/feedback accepts any missionId with no ownership check
No middleware.ts — no security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, etc.)

critical_context_to_read.purpose: Lines 1-3 have the TODO comment about Upstash Redis rate limiting. This is the most expensive endpoint (~$0.05/call to Anthropic). Currently has ZERO rate limiting. Read first 30 lines to understand the setup, then lines 700-786 for the POST handler entry point.

Existing Supabase-based rate limiter used by /api/mission. Uses RPC check_rate_limit. Fails open on error (line 57). This pattern should be reused for /api/verdict with the same 10/day limit, but verdict route currently does NOT call it.
Lines 3-15: Admin auth uses ADMIN_KEY from env, compared via query param. Must move to Authorization header.
45 lines, zero auth. Accepts arbitrary event_name, owner_token, thread_id, metadata. Must add basic validation.
55 lines. Zod validation is good but no ownership check — accepts any missionId. Low risk but should validate.

implementation_tasks.title: Add rate limiting to /api/verdict
changes: At the top of the POST handler (after input validation, before Anthropic API call), extract IP from x-forwarded-for header and owner_token from request body or cookies
Call checkRateLimit(ip, ownerToken). If not allowed, return 429 with error code 'RATE_LIMITED', message 'Rate limit exceeded. Maximum 10 verdicts per day.', and resetsAt ISO string
Remove the TODO comment on lines 1-3 (it's now done)
implementation_tasks.code_pattern: Copy the exact pattern from src/app/api/mission/route.ts lines 48-54 — that endpoint already uses checkRateLimit correctly. The verdict route should use the same approach.
implementation_tasks.test: Submit 11 verdicts from same IP within 24h. The 11th should return 429.

implementation_tasks.title: Move admin auth from query param to Authorization header
changes: Replace line 7 `const key = url.searchParams.get('key')` with `const auth = req.headers.get('authorization')` and `const key = auth?.startsWith('Bearer ') ? auth.slice(7) : null`
Keep the same ADMIN_KEY comparison on line 13
Delete the `const url = new URL(req.url)` line (no longer needed for auth)
Add timing-safe comparison: use crypto.timingSafeEqual instead of !== to prevent timing attacks. Import from 'crypto'. Convert both strings to Buffer before comparing.
implementation_tasks.notes: After this change, admin requests must use: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer <key>' /api/admin/stats. Document this in the reporting format.

implementation_tasks.title: Add basic validation to /api/events
changes: Add a Zod schema for event validation: event_name must be a string with max 100 chars from an allowed list, owner_token optional string, thread_id optional string uuid, mission_id optional string uuid, metadata optional object with max 5 keys

The allowed event names list: ['verdict_requested', 'verdict_completed', 'verdict_shared', 'verdict_tweeted', 'feedback_submitted', 'link_copied', 'page_view', 'history_cleared', 're_evaluate']. Reject unknown event names.

Add rate limiting: max 100 events per hour per IP (use a simple in-memory Map with TTL — events are fire-and-forget, not worth a DB call). This is acceptable because events are low-value and losing some on cold start is fine.
Return 400 on validation failure, 429 on rate limit
implementation_tasks.notes: Events are fire-and-forget analytics. The goal is to prevent spam/poisoning, not to add full auth. Keep the implementation simple.

implementation_tasks.title: Add Next.js middleware with security headers
changes: Create src/middleware.ts (Next.js middleware runs at the edge before every request)

Add security headers to all responses: X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, X-DNS-Prefetch-Control: on, Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()
Do NOT add CSP yet — it requires careful tuning with inline scripts/styles and can break the app. Flag as future improvement.
Export a config.matcher that excludes static files: ['/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|icon.svg|.*\.svg$).*)']
implementation_tasks.notes: Keep middleware minimal. Its only job is security headers. Do NOT add auth logic here — that stays in individual route handlers.

implementation_tasks.title: Add robots.txt and basic sitemap
implementation_tasks.robots_txt_content:
User-agent: *
Allow: /
Disallow: /api/
Disallow: /admin/
Disallow: /eval
Disallow: /lab/

Sitemap: https://councilpro.vercel.app/sitemap.xml
implementation_tasks.sitemap_content: Export a default function that returns an array of { url, lastModified, changeFrequency, priority } objects. Include: / (priority 1.0), /how-we-score (priority 0.5). Do NOT include /api/*, /admin/*, /lab/*, /eval, /v/[id] (dynamic), /thread/[id] (dynamic).
implementation_tasks.notes: Use Next.js built-in sitemap generation (app/sitemap.ts). See Next.js docs for MetadataRoute.Sitemap type.

implementation_tasks.title: Add minimal privacy and terms pages
design: Both pages: server components (no 'use client'), simple prose layout with max-w-2xl mx-auto px-6 py-16
Use the same header (AppHeader or inline Council link + ThemeToggle) as other pages
Content should be minimal but legally functional — what data is collected (idea text, analytics events, feedback), how it's stored (Supabase), no data selling, contact email for deletion requests
Terms: service provided as-is, not financial/legal advice, AI verdicts are opinions not guarantees
Add links to /privacy and /terms in the footer of page.tsx (next to existing GitHub link)
copy_guidelines: Keep tone consistent with Council — direct, no legalese
Privacy: 'We store your idea text to generate a verdict. We track anonymous analytics events. We don't sell your data. Email <founder-email> to request deletion.'
Terms: 'Council provides AI-generated opinions about startup ideas. These are not investment advice. Use at your own judgment.'
implementation_tasks.notes: These don't need to be perfect legal documents — they need to exist for launch credibility. Founder can iterate with a lawyer later.

what_not_to_build: Do NOT add full OAuth/JWT auth system — overkill for current stage
Do NOT add CSP headers — requires careful tuning, can break inline styles/scripts
Do NOT add Upstash Redis — existing Supabase rate limiting is sufficient for launch volume
Do NOT refactor rate-limit.ts — it works, just needs to be called from verdict route
Do NOT add CORS middleware — Vercel handles this correctly by default for same-origin
Do NOT modify the verdict API logic, prompt, or scoring — security only
Do NOT add user accounts or session management
Do NOT add request logging or Sentry in this spec — that's a separate concern
acceptance_criteria: AC1: POST /api/verdict returns 429 after 10 requests from same IP within 24h
AC2: GET /api/admin/stats returns 401 without Authorization header. Returns 200 with correct Bearer token.
AC3: GET /api/admin/stats?key=<value> (old query param style) returns 401 — old auth method no longer works
AC4: POST /api/events with unknown event_name returns 400
AC5: POST /api/events with valid event_name returns 200
AC6: All responses include X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy headers
AC7: GET /robots.txt returns correct content with Disallow for /api/ and /admin/
AC8: GET /sitemap.xml returns valid XML with / and /how-we-score
AC9: /privacy and /terms pages render with correct content
AC10: Footer links to /privacy and /terms exist
AC11: No TypeScript errors, build passes, no console errors
AC12: Existing verdict flow, history, sharing, feedback all work unchanged

manual_test_plan.steps: Use curl to send 11 POST requests to /api/verdict with a valid idea. Count responses.
manual_test_plan.expected: First 10 return 200 (or normal error). 11th returns 429 with resetsAt timestamp.

manual_test_plan.steps: Submit a verdict, check history, share it, give feedback
manual_test_plan.expected: All flows work exactly as before

reporting_format:
